## About the author

Follow authors to get new release updates, plus improved recommendations.

![Alex Hormozi](https://m.media-amazon.com/images/S/amzn-author-media-prod/eov911dsc81vaj73li2pag9pt7._SY600_._SL200_._PQ50_._FMwebp_.jpg)

[**Alex Hormozi**](https://www.amazon.com/Alex-Hormozi/e/B099H3YM3R/ref=aufs_dp_mata_dsk)

Brief content visible, double tap to read full content.

Full content visible, double tap to read brief content.

Alex Hormozi is an entrepreneur, investor, and author of three bestselling books. He and his wife Leila are managing partners of Acquisition. com, a private equity firm that invests in cash flow positive, founder-owned businesses. Their portfolio companies generate over 200 Million Dollars annually as of 2024. Their initial success came from packaging and licensing their boutique gym model to 5000+ gyms worldwide, followed by launching complementary supplement and software companies. In 2021, they sold a 66% stake to American Pacific Group at 46.2 Million Dollar valuation.

The couple now focuses on making real business education accessible to everyone through social media content, books, and free courses for entrepreneurs.

Alex's books, including "100M Offers: How to Make Offers So Good People Feel Stupid Saying No," have sold over 1,000,000+ copies around the world. In their spare time, Alex and his wife still enjoy training at hardcore gyms and fending off death one workout at a time.

##### _5.0 out of 5 stars_Must Read Book to understand the working model of a service based industry/ product/market



Reviewed in India on February 8, 2025

Format: Kindle [Verified Purchase](https://www.amazon.com/gp/help/customer/display.html/ref=cm_cr_dp_d_rvw_avp?nodeId=G8UYX7LALQC8V9KA)






\[book:Gym Launch Secrets: The Step-By-Step Guide To Building A Massively Profitable Gym\|51007045\]



If you are interested in understanding, going in depth of the working model of a service based industry, read this! If you are a Gym/Yoga/ Health/Fitness business owner, entrepreneur, trainer, read this! If you a tech product manager, a b2b SaaS, AI product manager or a product marketing manager, read this!



Alex distills his 6 to 7 year journey knowledge in this book and the mistakes one could make so that you don't. Every business model, domain has specific levers of growth. Understanding them gives you a data point in your latticework of mental models enriching you with transferrable knowledge to apply not just in concerned domain but other business domains as well. Service based industry has its own charm, its own loop holes, its own major challenges in maximizing revenue while still keeping the human in the loop element intact. As with any product/service, service based product has a lot of commonalities with other business models such as 1)ecommerce 2)SaaS-subscription 3)Freemium 4)User generated content with ads(Meta, Instagram) 5)Market Place products.



Below are some concepts that Alex distills, you may feel you know them, but probably you have actually just heard them and not understood them in its entirety. Read, if you feel you actually know, skip the pages, if there is a concept being encountered for the first time, read it, understand it.



● The Broken Acquisition System ❍❍ Client-Financed Acquisition ❍❍ Irresistible Offers

❍❍ Lead Nurture ❍❍ Sales

![](https://cdn.prod.website-files.com/63e6c1e86653498b2e3849c1/647f0cef3d80082311b61150_dI1bop0JRh6w54u7IXo3_bookstack_paul.png)

![](https://cdn.prod.website-files.com/63e6c1e86653498b2e3849c1/647f635d05e8c914e7a02cf3_Amz_Stars.png)

Insightful & Informative

By **Amazon Customer** on December 15, 2021 🇺🇸

I enjoyed reading and listening to Alex’s success stories. He overcame a ton and this book teaches you step by step how not to be broke! Buy it!

![](https://cdn.prod.website-files.com/63e6c1e86653498b2e3849c1/647f635d05e8c914e7a02cf3_Amz_Stars.png)

**Must for all gym owners**

By **Amazon Customer** on October 14, 2021 🇬🇧

Practical tips from someone that's been there and done it..... Thousands of times. Newbies and experienced gym owners can learn something from this.

[CLAIM MY COPY ON AMAZON](https://www.gymlaunch.com/secrets#)

# **INSIDE Gym Launch Secrets**

# **Learn The EXACT Models to Get More Clients**

- **Use the 8-Step Irresistible Offer Formula** to make your membership offer nearly impossible to say “no” to, without even changing your service. (Honestly, this makes sales simple for people who hate sales.)

- **Client Financed Acquisition** is essentially getting your current customers to pay for your marketing budget. This is how our gyms use "other people's money" to scale rapidly and make "marketing budgets" virtually a thing of the past.

- **Our proven Lead Generation Scrambler** that shows you how to create never-ending high-converting ad variations WITHOUT expensive software or prior marketing experience.

- **The "Icing" Front End Cash Process** that teaches you how to make money WHILE customers "try-out" your gym.

- **The "Thomas Jefferson" Trainer Hack** to teach your trainers how to upsell all your members FOR YOU


![](https://cdn.prod.website-files.com/63e6c1e86653498b2e3849c1/647f1f69e0d0c64b0795a377_mockup-of-a-paperback-book-lying-on-a-transparent-surface-a15680.png)[CLAIM MY COPY ON AMAZON](https://www.gymlaunch.com/secrets#)

# **Learn How to Earn More From Your Current Clients**

- **The 3 "Profit Maximizing" Price Levers...** you can use to learn how to repackage your membership & billing cycles TOMORROW & make more per customer. (Seriously, just one of them can increase your yearly revenue by 7.7% without a single change in fulfillment)

- **The Money Math Gym Formula** I literally spent years figuring this out with my 6 gyms. It is the formula that teaches normal gyms how to be Super Gyms. Don't run your gym another day without it.

- **Our infamous "Rate Increase Letter"...** This single letter will teach you how to add thousands to your EFT.

- **The "Bolt-On" Revenue System…** Learn how to tack on as much as $200 per month per customer. Simply by adding 2 obvious questions to your onboarding process (that most of them say YES to anyway)

- **The $600/mo "One-Yes" Upsell** you can use to convert your best customers into cash cows…


[CLAIM MY COPY ON AMAZON](https://www.gymlaunch.com/secrets#)

# **Learn How to Minimize Churn and Maximize the Lifetime Value of Your Clients**

- **The 5 Horsemen of Retention...** Discover these 5 simple processes to help keep your clients coming back while dramatically decreasing churn.

- **The 4-Layered "Wedding Cake" Gym Model...** that teaches gyms how to go from a fixed model to 6 Figures+ a month in revenue.

- **Get the Highest Converting Staff Ads for managers, trainers, salespeople and admins…** These are the EXACT ads I used when I ran my gym. Honestly, top talent can be had for a lot less that most would imagine.

- **The "Boring But Rich" Blueprint** that teaches gyms how to MAXIMIZE profits and keep them consistent

- **The One Page Team Template that gives every employee a roadmap** to over deliver to your customers with cyborg-like consistency (this is how we MASSIVELY scaled our company).


[CLAIM MY COPY ON AMAZON](https://www.gymlaunch.com/secrets#)

![](https://cdn.prod.website-files.com/63e6c1e86653498b2e3849c1/647f2ce739fa552032ac171f_Stars.svg)

ABOUT US

# GYM LAUNCH

Gym Launch is a movement for all gym owners to reach more people, change more lives, and build wildly profitable businesses.

Founded in 2016 by gym owners, We've worked with over 6,000 gyms across 22 countries, creating over one hundred $1M/yr gyms and helping thousands of gym owners take home $100,000/yr or more. We've accomplished this through our proprietary Expert Coaching and Education model.

In the fiercely competitive world of publishing, where more than [4 million books](https://stevelaube.com/four-million-books-published-each-year/) are released annually in the U.S. alone, the harsh reality for most authors is not the agony of writing the manuscript but the silence that follows its release, as they do not understand how to market a book.

Consider the numbers: The average self-published book sells [fewer than 250 copies](https://ideas.bkconnection.com/10-awful-truths-about-publishing) over its lifetime, according to data, while even traditionally published titles often fail to exceed 3,000 units in their first year, per industry analyses from Publishers Weekly.

These figures underscore a systemic failure not in creativity or content quality, but in promotion—the invisible machinery that turns a book from a solitary creation into a cultural or commercial phenomenon.

Enter [Alex Hormozi](https://www.acquisition.com/bio-alex), the entrepreneur whose August 16, 2025, launch of $100M Money Models shattered these conventions and set a new benchmark for what promotion can achieve.

**In just 24 hours, Hormozi sold more than 2.7 million copies, generating over $81 million in revenue and clinching the Guinness World Record for the most nonfiction books sold in a single day**—eclipsing previous marks by figures like Prince Harry, whose memoir moved 1.4 million units on debut.

He incurred zero dollars in ad spend, relying instead on organic reach, affiliate partnerships, and a “movement” framing that encouraged bulk purchases as donations to aspiring entrepreneurs.

The launch featured two marathon live streams on YouTube, each exceeding six hours, blending in-depth teaching with irresistible bonuses like AI advisors and exclusive workshops valued at up to $200,000.

Hormozi’s triumph exposes the flaws in conventional promotion while offering a blueprint for reform. By prioritizing audience trust over immediate sales, stacking perceived value through tiered bundles (e.g., buying 200 copies for $6,000 unlocked premium perks), and creating urgency via limited-time offers, he transformed a book launch into a high-stakes event that drew 100,000 live viewers and sparked viral buzz.

This approach netted not just record sales but a self-sustaining ecosystem of advocates, proving that promotion isn’t about pushing product—it’s about pulling in people with genuine utility.

In the sections ahead, we’ll dissect Hormozi’s playbook step by step, from pre-launch hype to post-event momentum, and scale it down for authors at any level.

## 1\. How To Promote Your Book: Build Before You Sell

![A packed audience listens to two speakers discuss strategies on stage at an international book fair, highlighting insights on how to promote your book through events, community, and reader engagement.](https://strategicpete.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/08/shutterstock_2452845925.jpg)_The key to earning attention lies in giving away content that provides immediate, tangible value._

The most potent weapon an author wields is an audience cultivated long before the manuscript hits the presses. Traditional publishing lore often romanticizes the solitary writer, toiling in isolation until a [finished book](https://strategicpete.com/podcasts/how-to-market-your-book-before-its-even-finished-with-peter-murphy-lewis/) magically attracts readers through sheer merit.

Yet, industry data paints a starkly different picture: Books without a pre-existing platform rarely break through, with [90% of titles selling fewer than 2,000 copies in their lifetime](https://www.elysian.press/p/no-one-buys-books), according to a book research report.

This grim statistic underscores a fundamental truth: when it comes to how to promote your book, **promotion begins not at launch but years earlier, through deliberate brand-building that transforms an unknown scribe into a trusted voice.**

For authors interested in learning how to market a book, this means shifting from creator to communicator, investing in content that fosters loyalty and positions the book as an inevitable extension of an ongoing conversation rather than a cold solicitation.

Alex Hormozi exemplifies this principle, having amassed an audience of over 9 million across platforms by August 2025—3.55 million subscribers on YouTube, 4 million followers on Instagram, 918,000 on X (formerly Twitter), and 714,000 on LinkedIn—without spending a dime on ads for his record-breaking launch of $100M Money Models.

His ascent began not with sales pitches but with a “nothing to sell” ethos, a strategy he detailed in videos like his 2022 YouTube upload “How I Built a 1.2M Follower Audience in 6 Months,” which has garnered over 1 million views and emphasizes giving away high-value business insights for free to build trust and reciprocity.

Drawing from his own entrepreneurial struggles—growing Gym Launch from nearly going bankrupt to a $46 million exit in 2021—Hormozi shared practical advice on customer acquisition, offers, and leads, presenting it as philanthropy rather than a prelude to commerce.

This approach, as he explained in a 2024 podcast appearance on “ [The Game](https://open.spotify.com/show/6YNopzKDGDwf0auIpPTIID),” created a “cult-like following” where audiences felt indebted, priming them for future offers like his books.

By consistently delivering content that solved real problems—such as breakdowns of revenue models or scaling frameworks—without immediate upsells, Hormozi eroded skepticism in an era where most consumers distrust branded content.

The result? A loyal base that propelled his book to $2.9 million sales in 24 hours, driven by organic shares and word-of-mouth rather than paid campaigns.

Central to Hormozi’s playbook is **prioritizing platforms that amplify long-form expertise and foster community, with YouTube, podcasts, and X standing out as high-impact channels for authors seeking similar traction.** YouTube, where Hormozi’s channel boasts 781 million total views as of August 2025, serves as his cornerstone, hosting over 3,600 videos on business growth that average 200,000 views each.

He attributes this to a “quantity over perfection” mantra, outlined in his March 2025 LinkedIn article, where he advocates posting daily or near-daily content to outpace algorithms and build momentum, a tactic that caused his subscribers to surge by 10,000 in a single day in early August.

Podcasts, like his “The Game” series with over 500 episodes by mid-2025, allow for deeper dives—interviews with entrepreneurs yielding insights on failure and scaling—that [convert listeners into subscribers at a higher rate than short-form content](https://socialmediapro.com/blog/the-revival-of-long-form-why-micro-content-is-losing-to-depth-in-2025/).

X, meanwhile, functions as his rapid-fire engagement hub, with threads on topics like “money models” garnering 50,000 impressions per post, enabling real-time interaction that humanizes the brand and drives traffic to longer content.

For authors, the lesson is selective focus: **Choose 2-3 platforms aligned with your genre—YouTube for nonfiction explainers, podcasts for narrative-driven genres like memoir, X for timely debates—rather than spreading thin across TikTok or Facebook**, where Hormozi ranks them lower in his 2024 “Social Media Tier List” for sustained audience growth.

**The key to earning attention lies in giving away content that provides immediate, tangible value,** a Hormozi hallmark that authors can replicate by teasing book themes without spoiling the core product.

In his 55-minute YouTube masterclass on social media strategy, viewed 1.5 million times since 2022, Hormozi stresses “leading with value”—sharing frameworks, case studies, and proverbs from his books for free to demonstrate expertise and hook viewers.

For instance, pre-launch clips from $100M Money Models dissected cash flow pitfalls, solving entrepreneurs’ pain points and building anticipation.

Authors should mirror this by creating “gateway content”: Blog posts or videos unpacking one chapter’s key idea, infographics summarizing research, or X threads posing questions your book answers.

Crucially, make it audience-centric—Hormozi shifted in 2023 from broad appeals to targeting business owners specifically, boosting engagement by 40%, as he revealed in a 2025 YouTube update.

Avoid self-promotion; instead, aim for 80% education and 20% subtle calls-to-action, like email sign-ups for more tips, fostering a sense of community where followers become advocates.

This pre-built audience amounts to free distribution, as shown by Hormozi’s zero-ad-spend launch that depended on his nine million-strong network for virality, with affiliates and shares expanding reach to generate $81 million in sales.

For those starting from scratch, a structured **90-day organic game plan** can lay the foundation, drawing from proven frameworks:

- **Days 1–30: Focus on Clarity**
  - Define your ideal reader (e.g., aspiring entrepreneurs for business books)
  - Set up profiles on YouTube and X
  - Post daily value-driven content (five videos or threads per week)
  - Engage with similar creators to build reciprocity
- **Days 31–60: Emphasize Consistency**
  - Launch a weekly podcast or newsletter
  - Collaborate on guest posts
  - Track metrics like engagement rates
  - Aim for 1,000 followers through organic SEO tactics (e.g., keyword-optimized titles)
- **Days 61–90: Scale with Momentum**
  - Host live Q&As
  - Incentivize shares with free resources
  - Refine strategy based on analytics
  - Work toward 5,000 engaged followers—enough for a modest launch, as seen in indie authors who grew audiences from zero to book deals via similar plans

Hormozi’s journey proves that building before selling isn’t optional—it’s the difference between obscurity and a blockbuster.

## 2\. Pre-Launch Book Promotion Ideas: Building Hype

![Hands holding an e-reader displaying a digital book on a wooden table, symbolizing modern publishing and creative book promotion ideas for authors in the digital age.](https://strategicpete.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/08/shutterstock_2602472293.jpg)_Central to building momentum for your book is constructing an email list, the lifeblood of any launch, which Hormozi supercharged through a free registration funnel._

The weeks leading up to a book launch are a crucial turning point in publishing, where anticipation can either fade into indifference or explode into a frenzy that drives sales to new heights, thanks to excellent execution of book promotion ideas.

Hormozi’s pre-launch blueprint, initiated precisely six weeks prior on July 2, 2025, began with a purposeful announcement that framed the book not as a product but as a communal crusade against entrepreneurial pitfalls like cash flow shortages.

![ad 4nxdfyk5mbier8zwrsdt4avnvxxhmziazwuihpihnd8bl4ww5bpsz06si3wpqzbgipntl9b7oukcgbaevymsfjzdqivubqfold6tjemqdv6p4m6ph czols17v2pqdsgvedrpz490zg](https://strategicpete.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/08/AD_4nXdFYK5mBiER8ZwRSDT4AvnVxXhMziazwuIhpIHND8BL4WW5BPSz06Si3wPqZBGIPntl9B7OukCgbAevYmsFjzdQiVUBQfoLD6TJEMqDV6p4M6PH_czoLs17V2PqdSGVedRPz490zg.png)

[Source](https://x.com/AlexHormozi/status/1940410188462531011)

This wasn’t casual hype; it was narrative engineering, echoing his earlier successes with $100M Offers and $100M Leads, where similar framing helped scale his audience to 9 million followers.

By invoking a “movement” to equip 32.5 million U.S. entrepreneurs with tools for unbreakable business models, Hormozi tapped into aspirational psychology, making participation feel altruistic and urgent.

Want to know how to market a book by using similar hype? You can replicate this by **crafting announcements that solve a singular, acute pain point**—such as “overcoming writer’s block for good” in a creativity guide—and broadcasting them via email blasts or social media, ideally with a video.

**Start four to six months out if possible, but compress to six weeks by leveraging existing networks,** as Hormozi did, teasing “fireworks” and a secret two-year project exclusive to live attendees to stoke curiosity.

Central to building momentum for your book is **constructing an email list, the lifeblood of any launch, which Hormozi supercharged through a free registration funnel.**

Visitors signed up for the virtual event at no cost, but a $30 pre-order upgraded them to VIP status, unlocking four “amazing free bonuses”—including an undisclosed “absurd” fourth one—designed to reward early action and inflate perceived value.

This tiered approach, reminiscent of his prior launches that netted 500,000 sign-ups for $100M Leads, generated an estimated 250,000 registrants by leveraging opt-in landing pages optimized for conversions.

For authors, the mechanics are straightforward yet powerful when it comes to how to promote your book :

- Use tools like **Mailchimp** or **ConvertKit** to create a dedicated landing page with:

  - A compelling headline (e.g., _“Join the Movement: Unlock Your Book’s Secrets Early”_).
  - A simple signup form.
  - Lead magnets (e.g., free chapter PDF, webinar invite).
- Promote via:
  - Social bios and pinned posts.
  - Cross-platform links.
- Goal: **1,000 subscribers in the first week** via organic shares.

  - Example: Hormozi’s July 2 post drove thousands by promising exclusivity.

Nurture the list with automated sequences: A welcome email recapping the announcement, followed by bi-weekly updates building suspense, which can boost open rates to 30-40%, according to Prefinery’s pre-launch best practices.

If starting small, **offer incentives like discounted pre-orders or bonus content to convert free sign-ups into paid commitments**, mirroring Hormozi’s VIP model that funneled users toward bulk bundles later.

**Content creation during this window serves as the hype’s fuel**, with Hormozi deploying a steady stream of teasers to educate and entice. From July 2 through mid-August, he shared short clips on X and Instagram reviewing chapter proverbs, highlighting pain points like failing business models, and previewing the book’s four-part framework for cash flow mastery.

A July 13 video, viewed over 198,000 times, humorously tied gym memes to money models, while a July 17 post reiterated the launch date and registration, amassing 286,000 impressions.

![ad 4nxflm9dcgp9c6m15i4xuon47ioidxnhab c7jkbklrtmgwjie1 8oi9afsi3qvybylebq kdw2fsvpcklnr1oroc 38z t1bvp7uemg4iy7dy7kfk0gsyz hx7gwiv6l6catqexamq](https://strategicpete.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/08/AD_4nXfLM9dcgp9c6M15i4xuoN47iOiDXNHaB-c7JKBKLRTMgwjiE1-8OI9afSI3QVYbyleBq_kdw2FSvpCklnR1orOC-38z_T1BVP7uEmG4Iy7dY7Kfk0GsyZ_HX7gWiv6l6CatqEXaMQ.png)

Behind-the-scenes glimpses, such as his July 9 update on “two years of planning,” humanized the process and built emotional investment.

This drip-feed strategy aligns with Kindlepreneur’s launch guide, which recommends positioning the book as a targeted solution—e.g., “the antidote to cash-strapped scaling”—through mixed media: Blog posts unpacking one idea, social reels for visuals, and podcasts for depth.

**Authors should plan 2-3 posts weekly**, using tools like Canva for graphics or Loom for videos, **making sure each one ends with a** **call-to-action** like “Register now to claim your bonuses.”

Monitor engagement with analytics to improve—Hormozi’s posts averaged over 100,000 views, confirming his strategy of sharing value-first content that subtly promotes the book’s promise.

Finally, **strategic partnerships amplify reach exponentially, a tactic Hormozi mastered by recruiting affiliates and influencers** who promoted the launch to their audiences. Building on his $100M Leads affiliate program, which enlisted 12,600 partners, he incentivized shoutouts with commissions on bundle sales, leading to top affiliates like those announced post-launch who drove hundreds of copies each.

He prepped them via emails and a dedicated guide, encouraging shares without high-pressure tactics—some affiliates even waived earnings to build goodwill. Influencers in business niches, tapped through warm intros from his network, amplified via podcasts and posts, while cold outreach involved personalized DMs highlighting mutual benefits.

**For authors, start by listing 10-20 contacts—friends, fellow writers, podcasters—and pitch collaboratively**: “Share this teaser with your audience for a free copy and affiliate link.”

**Use platforms like HARO for media mentions or Reddit for niche communities**, avoiding annoyance by offering value first, such as guest posts.

Hormozi’s zero-ad model relied on this organic amplification, proving that six weeks of alliances can yield viral scale, turning hype into a self-perpetuating force.

## 3\. Not Just a Book: Crafting an Irresistible Offer

The secret to explosive success lies not in the pages themselves but in the packaging that surrounds them— **a meticulously crafted offer that transcends the mere exchange of cash for content.**

**Readers don’t buy ink and paper; they invest in transformation**, be it knowledge, status, or tangible results.

Alex Hormozi shattered this paradigm with his August 16, 2025, launch of $100M Money Models, engineering an offer so laden with value that it compelled buyers to purchase in bulk, donating excess copies to entrepreneurs while unlocking escalating perks—ultimately driving 2.9 million sales and $81 million in revenue within 24 hours.

His core philosophy, echoed in prior works like $100M Offers, posits the book as merely the “tip of the value iceberg,” a low-barrier entry point to a cascade of bonuses that amplify perceived worth, making refusal feel irrational.

This strategy, rooted in value equation principles—maximizing dream outcomes while minimizing effort and sacrifice—turned a $30 book into a gateway for high-ticket commitments, proving that irresistible offers are built on ethical stacking, tiered structures, and psychological pricing.

![ad 4nxfextefe18noju x8rvfrb50ae5efbibhysadjjivi2kyvr5tzcx5ehrkxxz5aepjeuzqjnmj12lurtn6ycmeejfebfrtlb7egs mvwnbuduvfjtnrb6ezu3ht62u8jfglhmmg3ig](https://strategicpete.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/08/AD_4nXfextEFE18NoJU-x8rVfRB50ae5efbIBHysAdJJiVI2kyVR5TzCx5EhrKxxz5aepJeuzqJnmJ12LurTn6ycmeejfEBFrTLB7egS_MVwNBuduvfJtnRb6eZU3Ht62u8JfGLhmMg3Ig.png)

**Hormozi’s approach hinges on reframing the sale**: _Don’t peddle a book; deliver an ecosystem of outcomes that address the buyer’s deepest needs, such as scalable cash flow for entrepreneurs._

In his July 2, 2025, announcement video, which has been viewed over 302,000 times, he hinted at the book’s focus on “getting more customers to spend more money, in less time, over and over again,” but the real hook was the promise of four “amazing free bonuses” for VIP upgraders who pre-ordered a single copy.

**This stacking ethic**—layering complementary assets like courses, templates, communities, and tools— **ethically inflates value without deception**, as each element ties directly to the book’s themes of business longevity and profitability.

**Authors can adapt this by brainstorming bonuses that extend their expertise**:

- For a self-help writer, offer downloadable worksheets or a private webinar;
- For a novelist, include character backstories or a reader community forum.

The key is alignment—bonuses must solve adjacent problems, boosting conversion rates when perceived as “must-haves.”

**Ethical stacking requires transparency**: **Disclose values carefully** (for example, Hormozi estimated his at $35,000–$200,000 based on previous consulting fees) and make sure deliverables are realistic, avoiding overpromising that could damage trust.

For authors without Hormozi’s scale, **creating tiers remains accessible**—starting with digital assets that require no large audience or team:

- **Pricing Tiers**
  - **Base book**: $15–$30
  - **Mid-tier bundle**: $50, including digital add-ons like e-templates or audio extras
  - **Premium tier**: $100+, offering personalized coaching calls or community access (e.g., via Discord)
- **Tools & Platforms**
  - **Gumroad** or **Teachable** makes setup simple and manageable.
  - Case studies from indie authors show **2x–5x revenue uplifts** through bundling.
- **Value Stacking 101**
  - Quantify each bonus’s worth (e.g., _“a $97 course for free”_).
  - Ensure the **total perceived value is 5–10x higher than the price**, a principle championed by [pricing strategist **Ramit Sethi**](https://www.smartpassiveincome.com/podcasts/psychology-selling-how-to-price/).

Underpinning it all is **pricing psychology, where Hormozi anchored high to make mid-tiers irresistible.** By contrasting bundles against $135,000 consulting fees, a $6,000 package felt like a steal—classic [anchoring bias](https://cxl.com/blog/pricing-experiments-you-might-not-know-but-can-learn-from/), where initial high numbers skew perception, boosting conversions by 20–30%, according to behavioral economics research from Dan Ariely.

Authors can employ similar tactics:

- Anchor against comparable high-value services (e.g., “worth a $500 seminar”),
- Offer flexible payments via Stripe and
- Limit availability to drive FOMO.

Hormozi’s $81 million haul validates this: In a saturated market, the irresistible offer isn’t about the book—it’s about the empire it unlocks.

## 4\. Launch Event Mechanics: How to Sell Without Selling

The climax of any book launch—the event itself—often determines whether a title fades into obscurity or surges to bestseller status, yet many authors approach it as a mere reading or Q&A, missing the opportunity to create an immersive experience that subtly drives conversions.

The key lies in a counterintuitive strategy: **selling without overt pressure, by prioritizing education and value delivery that builds trust and reciprocity**.

This “teach-to-sell” model, popularized in digital marketing circles, leverages psychological principles like the rule of reciprocity—where free insights compel recipients to reciprocate with purchases—resulting in higher close rates without the hard sell.

Webinar analytics shows that events blending 70% content with 30% offers achieve 40% better retention and 25% more upsells than pitch-heavy formats.

Alex Hormozi mastered this during his August 16, 2025, launch of $100M Money Models, hosting a marathon YouTube live stream that blended exhaustive teaching with seamless pitches, attracting over 100,000 viewers and generating $81 million in revenue while shattering the Guinness World Record for nonfiction book sales in 24 hours at 2.9 million copies.

His method, executed across two extended sessions (a primary 7- to 10-hour stream and a 6-hour encore), emphasized “selling without selling” by **front-loading value, interspersing testimonials**, and **framing buys as altruistic donations, turning the event into a movement rather than a transaction**.

Hormozi’s live launch strategy was a masterclass in endurance and engagement, eschewing short webinars for extended formats that allowed deep immersion, mirroring the intensive workshops he charges $35,000 for privately.

The main event, starting at 9 a.m. PT on August 16, spanned approximately 7 to 10 hours on YouTube—no Zoom required—drawing from a pre-registered list of over 250,000 via his acquisition.com funnel.

He structured it as a hybrid of seminar and infotainment, kicking off with high-energy teasers and festivities to hook viewers, then diving into the book’s core content: a four-part framework for building resilient money models that ensure cash flow by acquiring more customers who spend more, faster, repeatedly.

With over 1,500 slides guiding the presentation, Hormozi taught breakdowns of leads, conversions, lifetime value (LTV), and retention strategies, using real-world examples from his $200 million annual portfolio at Acquisition.com.

Interspersed were Q&A sessions via live chat, case studies—like 19-year-old Ava’s journey from zero to $48,000 monthly revenue—and guest appearances from successful entrepreneurs sharing how they applied his models, illustrating the book’s principles in action.

Breaks were built in to combat fatigue, maintaining viewer retention at peak levels, with real-time demos of tools like his custom AI advisor, trained on $31 million in consulting data from 1,026 businesses.

This format not only educated but positioned the book as indispensable, with Hormozi emphasizing, “A good money model gets you more customers, to spend more money, in less time, over and over again,” tying every segment back to the thesis.

What elevated the event was Hormozi’s ability to sell subtly, teaching first and pitching second, a tactic that fostered genuine buy-in.

Visual aids, such as physical playbooks and binders, were used to make value more tangible, while the AI advisor was demonstrated in real-time, answering questions based on his proprietary data.

For authors replicating this—even solo on Zoom or YouTube—the blueprint is clear:

- **Host a 1- to 3-hour virtual event** (scalable from Hormozi’s epic length), outlining your book’s core ideas as actionable sessions.
- **Use free tools like StreamYard for overlays, Canva for slides, and live chat for interaction,** ensuring 60-70% of the time is pure teaching to build authority before any ask.

**Urgency and scarcity were woven masterfully**, converting education into action without aggression. Pitches used donation framing—buy 200 books for $5,998, keep one, “donate” 199 to entrepreneurs—making purchases feel philanthropic while qualifying for bonuses worth $135,000+.

Real-time updates built FOMO: “Page goes down when stream ends,” with limited playbooks and expiring bonuses like six virtual intensives over six months, led by Hormozi, Leila, and Sharran Srivatsaa on marketing, sales, and wealth. Chat hype, leaderboards for affiliates (12,600 from prior launches), and live feedback loops amplified momentum, with phrases like “Decline this one-time offer forever” reinforcing scarcity.

**Authors can adapt by setting time-bound offers** (e.g., “Bonuses expire in 48 hours”), **using chat polls for engagement,** and **displaying counters to create viral urgency**, even in smaller events.

Hormozi’s encore stream on August 17, lasting about 6 hours in the afternoon, reinforced the formula for stragglers, recapping teachings, pitching bonuses, and extending the 24-hour record window via global time zones.

This two-pronged approach maximized reach, proving that launches thrive on persistence.

For indie authors, **an encore webinar the next day can capture missed traffic, using recordings to sustain momentum**.

## How To Market a Book: Lessons From the $81M Playbook (Even if You’re Not Hormozi)

![Crowd of readers browsing and selecting titles at a busy book fair, illustrating strategies on how to market a book through events and direct audience engagement.](https://strategicpete.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/08/shutterstock_382261924-1.jpg)_Self-published books now command 50% of the e-book market, per a 2025 Bowker report_.

Alex Hormozi’s orchestration of the $100M Money Models launch on August 16, 2025, wasn’t just a commercial triumph—it was a masterclass in entrepreneurial alchemy, converting years of free content into $81 million in revenue from over 2.9 million book sales within 24 hours, all while shattering the Guinness World Record for nonfiction titles.

This feat, achieved with zero advertising spend and a pre-built audience of 9 million followers, underscores a pivotal truth in publishing: **scale isn’t reserved for titans with vast resources; it’s replicable for independent authors willing to adapt core principles to their level**.

For anyone wondering how to market a book effectively, Hormozi’s playbook offers a blueprint that goes beyond hype to proven strategy.

Industry data reinforces this—self-published books now command 50% of the e-book market, per a 2025 Bowker report, yet success hinges on strategic execution rather than sheer size, with top indie earners averaging $100,000 annually by focusing on high-value offers and engaged niches, according to a Reedsy survey of 2,000 authors.

Hormozi’s approach, distilled from his journey scaling Acquisition.com to $200 million in annual revenue, delivers timeless lessons that democratize blockbuster launches.

**By emphasizing value over volume, leveraging accessible tools, and hacking creativity with AI**, even solo creators can engineer launches that punch above their weight, turning modest followings into sustainable income streams.

**The first scalable lesson is prioritizing value over sheer volume, a shift that empowers indie authors to compete without Hormozi’s multimillion-follower base.** At its core, his campaign succeeded by stacking “insane” bonuses—such as AI advisors and exclusive workshops valued at $135,000—onto book bundles, making each purchase feel like an investment in transformation rather than a transaction.

This value-first mindset, where the book serves as a low-entry gateway to deeper outcomes like business scaling frameworks, mirrors tactics used by successful indies like James Clear, whose Atomic Habits sold 20 million copies by bundling free habit trackers and newsletters that extended the book’s utility.

For smaller authors, this means focusing on quality deliverables over quantity: Instead of chasing viral hits, create bonuses like downloadable templates or mini-courses that directly solve reader pain points, such as plot outlining tools for fiction writers or SEO guides for nonfiction.

A 2025 study by the Alliance of Independent Authors (ALLi) found that indies incorporating value stacks see 2.5 times higher conversion rates, with 62% reporting sustained sales from repeat buyers drawn to the ecosystem rather than the book alone.

Hormozi’s donation framing—buyers “gifting” extra copies to entrepreneurs—added an altruistic layer, boosting perceived impact; indies can adapt this by encouraging bulk buys for book clubs or charities, fostering community and word-of-mouth without massive audiences.

**Crucially, Hormozi’s success proves you don’t need millions of followers—just a focused, engaged audience cultivated through consistent, niche-specific content.** His 4 million Instagram and 3.55 million YouTube subscribers were built over years with “nothing to sell” videos on business models, yielding organic reach that drove 100,000 live viewers to his stream.

For indies, this translates to targeting micro-niches: A romance author might nurture 5,000 email subscribers via Wattpad serials, while a tech writer builds 2,000 on Substack with code tutorials.

The key is depth over breadth—Hormozi’s followers felt part of a “movement,” as evidenced by 286,000 impressions on pre-launch posts; **indies achieve this by hosting free webinars or AMAs, converting lurkers into advocates and amplifying reach organically.**

To execute these lessons, **indie authors can harness affordable tools and tech that mirror Hormozi’s infrastructure, democratizing high-impact launches without enterprise budgets**.

Email platforms form the backbone: Hormozi used his list for reminders and upsells, but indies thrive with free tiers of Mailchimp (up to 500 subscribers) or ConvertKit (up to 1,000), which automate sequences like teaser drips and bonus deliveries, boosting open rates to 35-45% with segmentation features.

Community tools extend engagement—Hormozi’s exclusive Skool group for high-ticket buyers fostered networking; alternatives like Circle ($49/month) or free Discord servers enable indie authors to create VIP spaces for Q&A and beta reads, with ALLi reporting 40% higher retention among community-engaged readers.

For live events, YouTube Live (free) replicates his streams, while StreamYard ($20/month) adds professional overlays. Payment processors like Gumroad or Stripe handle bundles seamlessly, with no upfront costs beyond transaction fees.

**Hormozi-style AI hacks** further level the playing field, giving indie authors pro-level leverage without huge teams or budgets:

- **Content & Reader Engagement**
  - Use **ChatGPT (free tier)** for FAQs:

    - Prompt: _“Generate 10 reader questions and answers based on my book summary.”_
    - Benefits: Streamlines customer support and creates content for blogs, socials, or newsletters.
- **Video & Script Creation**
  - Tools like **Jasper** or **Grok AI** (~$20/month) can draft launch teasers.

    - Prompt: _“Write a 60-second script teasing chapter insights on \[topic\].”_
    - Impact: Indie authors report **50% faster content creation** (2025 Kindlepreneur survey).
- **Bonus & Value Stack Creation**
  - **Midjourney** or **DALL·E** (~$10/month) for custom visuals.
  - **Sudowrite** for generating worksheets, guides, or prompts.
  - Enables add-ons like AI-edited companion guides to enhance bundles.
- **Community & Automation**
  - Hormozi demoed AI’s real-time utility during his launch stream.
  - Indies can replicate with **Zapier integrations**, embedding bots into Discord/Slack to:

    - Automate responses.
    - Maintain engagement.
    - Foster loyalty.
- **Best Practices**
  - Always disclose AI use and **human-edit outputs** for quality.
  - Case study: **Hugh Howey**, who leveraged similar tech to self-publish _Wool_ into a multimillion-dollar series.

The key lesson from Hormozi’s $81 million playbook is to **promote as if your book is a $100** **million business, even if it’s your first**— **ambition and offer design, not audience size,** **determine results**.

By framing launches as movements, stacking value, and leveraging tools, indies can achieve outsized results:

A focused 1,000-person list with a compelling bundle can yield $10,000-$20,000, per PublishDrive data, scaling to six figures with iteration.

Hormozi’s record-breaking event, with its encore stream and post-launch momentum, reminds us that publishing success is engineered, not accidental. The difference lies in viewing your work not as a hobby but a high-stakes enterprise, where bold offers and relentless value delivery turn modest beginnings into enduring empires.

Slash marketing costs by 75%!

Peter Murphy Lewis drives KPI-focused strategies, executes with precision, and delivers measurable growth—hands-on.

Welcome to the

17 Money Models We Use To Build $20,000/mo Online Fitness Businesses Like Clockwork.

I want to directly say thank you for taking the time to pick up my little ebook. I know your attention is valuable, so I want to honor that by being as direct as possible with each of the concepts I would like to outline in this book. I’ll try to say as little as possible, but no less, so you can quickly understand & apply the concept in your own business (with as little consumption effort as possible).

What you have in your hands is the culmination of what we found across 378+ gym locations going online all at the same time (at the time of this writing).

The model you will discover in these pages is the model that many of our top 7F+ gym owners, PERMANENTLY abandoned their in person facilities for...to make MORE. Transparently, I called this ebook “...to Build $20,000/mo Online Fitness Businesses” but in reality, there is no cap. I only named it that since many in the fitness space have limiting beliefs about their earning potential. If I said 12 months to $1M/yr in PROFIT...no one would believe it, but this book describes a model that can do just that.

How this model was forged...

When Covid hit, we had a large base of gyms who needed to figure out an online revenue solution ASAP. And we needed to create a model ANYONE could execute, with little sales skills, little online coaching skills, & ZERO branding...OVERNIGHT...from paid ads. This was not a small task. And to be honest, no “guru” in the space had ever done this because...it’s hard. There are 3 prevailing models prior to this that are taught that allow people with 0 brand, authority, or following to make money in fitness online: 1)Direct Cold Messenger/chat/DM to phone sale (works but requires EFFORT)a)Most don’t have the discipline to do this consistently at scale2)Ad to Webinar To Paid Application to High Ticket Phone Salea)Requires far too many skills for someone starting out (note: 1 of 20 people whobuy courses on this absolutely CAN win with this, it’s just that MOST do not)3)Online 5-Day Challengesa)Ads push people to a free group. Guru makes videos daily to provide value.Makes offer day 3-4. Sells anyone who comments or messages about offer.b)Lots of repeated effort to get someone to give you money, difficult to scale.

![](https://html.scribdassets.com/75hfu5irr4beyo9o/images/3-53c904777e.png)

![](https://html.scribdassets.com/75hfu5irr4beyo9o/images/3-53c904777e.png)![](https://html.scribdassets.com/75hfu5irr4beyo9o/images/3-53c904777e.png)![](https://html.scribdassets.com/75hfu5irr4beyo9o/images/3-53c904777e.png)

ad [Download to read ad-free](https://www.scribd.com/oauth/signup?behavior_tag=download&doc_id=670206741)

Mind you, there are many success stories a guru in this space can march in front of you. And when people are purchasing a “system” from someone, they have little vested interest in their success. Winners win. Losers dont. And the world moves on. We have built the biggest niche coaching business (to my knowledge) over the past 4 years and stayed atop this industry because we have always taken a different approach.

What is the model that if executed could get 80-90% of people to be successful?

It is not: “

What did I do to be successful?” “What have some people done to be successful?”

And therein lies the difference between our approach and most everyone else in the industry. We ask...

“How easy can we make this?” “How FEW skills can we require within our business model in order to make it work?” “How LITTLE capital (cash) can we require to get someone to win?”

By doing this we create more confines on our own thinking process, but the resulting model tends to be far more robust and replicable across businesses. They also tend to scale better because if it doesn't require a lot of skill to learn...then it means you can pay someone else to do it for you…which makes it a...

REAL business

:) That being said, we did not have this mythical model the moment COVID hit. We had the fulfillment, we just didn't have the acquisition portion down (as people could no longer sell in person). So we began experimenting...FAST. The advantage we had is that every gym in our community was fighting for their lives. So they all took massive action. With this dedication, we were able to systematically test every aspect of the model, in rapid succession, until we had THIS model (the one in your hands). No doubt - this model will continue to be improved upon.

![](https://html.scribdassets.com/75hfu5irr4beyo9o/images/4-8c06eeb8ad.jpg)

ad [Download to read ad-free](https://www.scribd.com/oauth/signup?behavior_tag=download&doc_id=670206741)

But for now, I can state with confidence that this model is the most direct path to making $200k/yr INCOME for any fitness professional.  And for the enterprising entrepreneur...far far more. Work from wherever you want…whenever you want...with people from wherever...and never struggle to get leads ever again. Welcome to the new world. Let’s dive in.

metadata.description: Online Gym Model Gym Launch Secrets - Free download as PDF File (.pdf), Text File (.txt) or read online for free. This document outlines a 17-step model for building a $20,000 per month online fitness business using paid advertising. It discusses getting clicks, converting clicks to leads, scheduling appointments, obtaining payment information, selling supplemental products, providing results, and converting clients to recurring memberships. The goal is to systematically test the model to find the most direct and replicable process that requires few skills and little capital from fitness professionals and entrepreneurs.

web.description: How did Alex Hormozi make $100 Million in just 24 hours? In this video, we break down the record-breaking book launch of Alex Hormozi's 100 ...
web.markdown:
![Thumbnail (336x188)](https://i.ytimg.com/vi/hw6QcXOQkV0/hqdefault.jpg?sqp=-oaymwEnCNACELwBSFryq4qpAxkIARUAAIhCGAHYAQHiAQoIGBACGAY4AUAB&rs=AOn4CLCU4DwSw5VzdqnHSh6nwOmZ2ifPEQ)
# [How Hormozi’s Book Launch Broke "Selling"](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=hw6QcXOQkV0)

**Visibility**: Public
**Uploaded by**: [Blueprint to Billions](http://www.youtube.com/@BlueprintToBillions2)
**Uploaded at**: 2025-08-20T13:30:11-07:00
**Published at**: 2025-08-20T13:30:11-07:00
**Length**: 10:32
**Views**: 20370
**Likes**: 933
**Category**: People & Blogs

## Description

```
🚀 How did Alex Hormozi make $100 Million in just 24 hours?
In this video, we break down the record-breaking book launch of Alex Hormozi’s 100 Million Money Models and reveal the exact psychological tactics, positioning strategies, and marketing blueprint he used to pull off one of the most legendary launches ever.

📚 From a live stream event to setting a world record for the most non-fiction books sold in 24 hours, Alex Hormozi didn’t just sell books—he built a masterclass in marketing, branding, and audience psychology.

✨ Watch as we cover:

The strategy behind Hormozi’s $100M launch

How he positioned his book for maximum impact

The psychological triggers he used to drive sales

How a single live stream turned into a $100M+ day

Lessons YOU can apply for your own business, brand, or product launch

If you’re an entrepreneur, marketer, or just curious about how the biggest names make history, this breakdown is for you.

👉 Don’t forget to like, share, and subscribe for more deep dives into business strategies, marketing psychology, and growth hacks!
```

## Transcript

Yesterday, a 35-year-old former gym owner just broke a Guinness World Record and generated $87.5 million in 8 hours, doing something that should be impossible. Alex Hormoszi sold 2.9 million copies of his book in 24 hours while hitting peak viewership of 113,000 people watching him live on YouTube. Yeah, you heard that right. He made more money in one day than most Fortune 500 companies make in a quarter. And he did it selling a $30 book. But here's the plot twist that'll make your brain do mental gymnastics. Most people think this was just a book launch setup. The impossible math. So, let's start with the numbers that sound completely made up. Because honestly, when I first saw these figures, I thought someone had accidentally added extra zeros to everything. Alex Hermosy's 100m Money Models book launch happened on August 16th, 2025. Literally yesterday as I'm recording this, he officially broke the Guinness World Record by selling 2,917,443 books in 24 hours. That's not a typo. Almost 3 million books in one day. But here's where your brain starts melting. Each book cost $30. So, we're looking at $87.5 million in revenue from what most people thought was just a book launch. To put this in perspective, that's more money than most companies on the New York Stock Exchange generate in an entire year. His live stream peaked at 113,000 concurrent viewers, which means more people were watching Alex talk about business models than most Super Bowl halftime shows get. And this went on for 10 straight hours. But wait, it gets crazier. Alex didn't just rely on people finding his book organically. He spent over $4 million on advertising alone, created 1,500 different ad creatives, and mobilized an army of 21,000 affiliates to promote this thing. It's like he built a military campaign disguised as a book launch. His previous book launches were already considered record-breaking. But this is like going from selling out Madison Square Garden to selling out the entire population of Chicago in one day. Part one, the three tier money machine. So, how do you make millions giving something away for free? Simple. You don't actually make money from the free thing. You make money from everything else. And Alex basically turned his book launch into the world's most sophisticated salesunnel disguised as a gift. Tier one, the free tier, the lost leader. Everyone got the book for free. Access to the live stream, some mystery secret project that sounds like it was named by a 12-year-old who watches too much Marvel and five guest speakers. This tier exists for one reason, to get people in the door. It's like Costco's $1.50 hot dog. They lose money on it, but you're not leaving without buying a 50 pack of toilet paper. Tier two, the VIP package. $29.99. The sweet spot. Here's where things get smart. For 30 bucks, you got a Vegas trip giveaway, guaranteed hardback copy, backstage live stream access, and here's the kicker. Recordings of the main event. Remember, Alex promised to you wipe the main event from the internet after it ended. So, if you wanted to watch it again, you had to upgrade. It's like hostage negotiation, but with book content. Tier three, the premium bundle, 5,998, The Whale Hunter. This is where the real money happened. For six grand, you got 12 physical playbooks, lifetime access to some AI trained on $31 million worth of consulting data, which sounds made up, but apparently isn't, and 200 books you could donate for tax write-offs. Now, $5,998 sounds insane for what's essentially a business course, until you realize his target audience is entrepreneurs who probably spend more than that on their monthly Starbucks habit. If just 1,000 people bought this bundle, which is entirely possible given his audience size, that's almost $6 million in revenue. And here's the beautiful part. All of this was sold after people had already received the free book and seen the value. It's like getting a free sample at Costco, except the sample is a full meal and then they convince you to buy the entire restaurant. Tier four, the ACQ Scale Advisory, $18,000, the Whale Hunter. But wait, there's more. After people bought the $6,000 bundle, they got offered the ACQ scale advisory program for $18,000. This included 6 months of virtual intensives with Alex's team, private community access, and ongoing support. Now, $18,000 sounds insane until you realize Alex's consulting normally runs $50,000 plus per engagement. So, this was positioned as a 60% discount for people who had already proven they were serious by buying the book bundle. If just 100 people bought this upsell, which is entirely possible given the scale, that's another $1.8 8 million in revenue on top of everything else. Part two, the affiliate army strategy. But here's where Alex really showed his genius. Instead of spending millions on Facebook ads like every other guru, he built an army of 23,000 affiliates to promote for him for free. Think about this for a second. Most book launches involve the author spending their own money on ads hoping to make it back in sales. Alex flipped this completely. He convinced 23,000 other people to spend their money promoting his book and somehow made them feel grateful for the opportunity. It's like if Tesla convinced every car dealer in America to promote Tesla cars using their own marketing budgets, except somehow that actually happened. The affiliate program was basically designed like a video game with achievement levels. Top 10 affiliates got in-person Vegas workshops, 30inut one-on-one sessions with Alex, and private dinners for business people trying to level up. That's basically the holy grail of networking opportunities. But Alex didn't just throw these affiliates into the wild and hope for the best. He gave them everything. Pre-written email sequences, ad copy templates, social media posts, even text message templates. It was like franchise marketing but for book promotion. The result, zero advertising spend for Alex while thousands of marketers tested different audiences and messaging for him. He basically crowdsourced the entire marketing campaign while getting detailed data on what works and what doesn't. It's like having access to AB test results from thousands of different marketing experiments, except you didn't pay for any of them. Part three, the psychological warfare. Now, the strategy gets really interesting when you look at the psychological tactics Alex used. This wasn't just a book launch. It was a master class in behavioral economics, the live business consultation. Genius. Here's where Alex pulled out what might be the smartest trust building move I've ever seen. Mid- liveream, he gave out a phone number and said, "Call this number right now and I'll solve your business problems live." And people actually did it. We're talking about a guy who probably charges $50,000 plus for private consulting, giving away free business advice to random callers on a live stream with a million people watching. The chat was going absolutely crazy with W after every piece of advice he gave. Think about the psychology here for a second. Instead of just talking about business theory, Alex was demonstrating his expertise in real time by solving actual business problems. It's like if Gordon Ramsay promised to teach you cooking, then actually cooked a five-star meal right in front of you while explaining every step, except the meal was someone's failing business, and Alex was the chef. The scarcity bomb. Alex promised to completely delete the main event recording after it ended. No replays, no second chances, no oops, I missed it options. This created what psychologists call loss aversion. People's fear of missing out often overrides their rational decision-making. It's like limited edition sneaker drops but for business content. The mystery box effect. That secret project he kept mentioning. Nobody knew what it was until the event. He literally convinced a million people to show up for a mystery gift. It's like if Amazon said, "Buy this box. We'll tell you what's in it later." And people actually did it, which knowing Amazon customers, they probably would. The social proof avalanche. With over a million registrations, Alex created massive social proof. When everyone's doing something, it must be important, right? It's the same psychology that makes people line up for hours for new iPhone releases, even though their old phone works perfectly fine. The authority transfer. By giving away valuable content for free first, Alex established credibility before asking for money. It's like a magician showing you a few tricks for free before asking you to pay for the full show. Except in this case, the free tricks were actually valuable business frameworks. The progressive commitment. First, people committed to registering for free. Then they committed time to attend. Then they were more likely to commit money to upgrade. It's like getting someone to agree to small requests before asking for bigger ones. Psychologists call this the foot in the door technique and Alex basically turned it into a money printing machine. Part four. Why this changes everything. Here's why every other business guru is probably having panic attacks right now. Alex just raised the bar so high that everyone else's launch strategy looks like they're selling lemonade at a Tesla dealership. Most authors do book launches like this. Write book, buy ads, hope people buy book, maybe break even. Alex did this. Create massive value, give it away free, build enormous audience, then sell premium implementation to people who are already convinced of the value. It's the difference between trying to convince strangers to buy something versus selling premium services to people who already know you deliver results. One is difficult. The other is just taking orders. But here's the kicker that's really got the industry shook. Alex proved you can scale authentic value delivery. Most people think you either provide great value to a small audience or mediocre value to a large audience. Alex delivered genuine value to over a million people simultaneously while generating massive revenue. The production investment alone, multiple millions of dollars, showed he was playing a completely different game. While other gurus are recording courses in their bedrooms, Alex basically created a Netflix level production for a book launch. And that affiliate strategy, it's not just smart marketing. It's building an entire distribution network. Those 23,000 affiliates aren't just promoting this launch, they're now part of his ecosystem for future launches. He basically built his own media empire disguised as a book promotion. The new playbook. So what does this all mean? Alex Horoszi didn't just launch a book yesterday. He launched a completely new business model that other entrepreneurs are going to be copying for years. The old model was create content, hope people buy it, repeat. The new model is create massive value, give it away strategically, build an ecosystem, then monetize the premium implementation. Whether you think Alex is a business genius or just really good at making gym advice sound profound, you can't argue with the results. The guy figured out how to make millions by giving things away. And that's a level of business jiu-jitsu that most people can't even comprehend. So the next time someone tells you that free content doesn't make money, just remind them that some former gym owner made $15 million in one day by giving away a book. Because apparently in 2025, the best way to sell something is to not sell it at all. And if you want to know more about how these business guru empires actually work, make sure you subscribe because trust me, this story is just getting started. What do you think? Is Alex Hormoszi a marketing genius or is this just a really expensive way to give away books? Let me know in the comments. And if you want more breakdowns of how these business empires actually make their money, hit that subscribe button.

Welcome to the

17 Money Models We Use To Build $20,000/mo Online Fitness Businesses Like Clockwork.

I want to directly say thank you for taking the time to pick up my little ebook. I know your attention is valuable, so I want to honor that by being as direct as possible with each of the concepts I would like to outline in this book. I’ll try to say as little as possible, but no less, so you can quickly understand & apply the concept in your own business (with as little consumption effort as possible).

What you have in your hands is the culmination of what we found across 378+ gym locations going online all at the same time (at the time of this writing).

The model you will discover in these pages is the model that many of our top 7F+ gym owners, PERMANENTLY abandoned their in person facilities for...to make MORE. Transparently, I called this ebook “...to Build $20,000/mo Online Fitness Businesses” but in reality, there is no cap. I only named it that since many in the fitness space have limiting beliefs about their earning potential. If I said 12 months to $1M/yr in PROFIT...no one would believe it, but this book describes a model that can do just that.

How this model was forged...

When Covid hit, we had a large base of gyms who needed to figure out an online revenue solution ASAP. And we needed to create a model ANYONE could execute, with little sales skills, little online coaching skills, & ZERO branding...OVERNIGHT...from paid ads. This was not a small task. And to be honest, no “guru” in the space had ever done this because...it’s hard. There are 3 prevailing models prior to this that are taught that allow people with 0 brand, authority, or following to make money in fitness online: 1)Direct Cold Messenger/chat/DM to phone sale (works but requires EFFORT)a)Most don’t have the discipline to do this consistently at scale2)Ad to Webinar To Paid Application to High Ticket Phone Salea)Requires far too many skills for someone starting out (note: 1 of 20 people whobuy courses on this absolutely CAN win with this, it’s just that MOST do not)3)Online 5-Day Challengesa)Ads push people to a free group. Guru makes videos daily to provide value.Makes offer day 3-4. Sells anyone who comments or messages about offer.b)Lots of repeated effort to get someone to give you money, difficult to scale.

![](https://html.scribdassets.com/75hfu5irr4beyo9o/images/3-53c904777e.png)

![](https://html.scribdassets.com/75hfu5irr4beyo9o/images/3-53c904777e.png)![](https://html.scribdassets.com/75hfu5irr4beyo9o/images/3-53c904777e.png)![](https://html.scribdassets.com/75hfu5irr4beyo9o/images/3-53c904777e.png)

ad [Download to read ad-free](https://www.scribd.com/oauth/signup?behavior_tag=download&doc_id=670206741)

Mind you, there are many success stories a guru in this space can march in front of you. And when people are purchasing a “system” from someone, they have little vested interest in their success. Winners win. Losers dont. And the world moves on. We have built the biggest niche coaching business (to my knowledge) over the past 4 years and stayed atop this industry because we have always taken a different approach.

What is the model that if executed could get 80-90% of people to be successful?

It is not: “

What did I do to be successful?” “What have some people done to be successful?”

And therein lies the difference between our approach and most everyone else in the industry. We ask...

“How easy can we make this?” “How FEW skills can we require within our business model in order to make it work?” “How LITTLE capital (cash) can we require to get someone to win?”

By doing this we create more confines on our own thinking process, but the resulting model tends to be far more robust and replicable across businesses. They also tend to scale better because if it doesn't require a lot of skill to learn...then it means you can pay someone else to do it for you…which makes it a...

REAL business

:) That being said, we did not have this mythical model the moment COVID hit. We had the fulfillment, we just didn't have the acquisition portion down (as people could no longer sell in person). So we began experimenting...FAST. The advantage we had is that every gym in our community was fighting for their lives. So they all took massive action. With this dedication, we were able to systematically test every aspect of the model, in rapid succession, until we had THIS model (the one in your hands). No doubt - this model will continue to be improved upon.

![](https://html.scribdassets.com/75hfu5irr4beyo9o/images/4-8c06eeb8ad.jpg)

ad [Download to read ad-free](https://www.scribd.com/oauth/signup?behavior_tag=download&doc_id=670206741)

But for now, I can state with confidence that this model is the most direct path to making $200k/yr INCOME for any fitness professional.  And for the enterprising entrepreneur...far far more. Work from wherever you want…whenever you want...with people from wherever...and never struggle to get leads ever again. Welcome to the new world. Let’s dive in.
